item.text: Those reads are still returning. The likely deck plan is Blast into Peddler, then spend the generated money on the strongest legal payload; board priority is to finish damaged P2 units and preserve P1’s center lead.

P2 cleared the prior unit-count threat by killing two P1 units and recruiting. I’m going to lean into the rush response: play Blast, Peddler, and the drawn Storm for maximum damage this turn, then use that to remove P2’s exposed damaged units if the board CLI permits the assignments.
The first source search hit a shell glob issue, so I’m rerunning it with fixed paths. This is just to understand how `stormTargets` is validated before I write the final board actions.

`stormTargets` is validated as part of deck-damage targeting, and the normal per-attacker cap still matters. I’m writing the actions now and will let the board runner compute exact damage and removals.

The board line is straightforward now: kill the 3 HP guardian with the marksman, kill the 2 HP west marksman with the adjacent raider, flip center-center, advance the eastern raiders, and recruit one raider from the six-center income. I’m keeping the board actions narrow so every movement directly supports pressure or a spawn.
The turn artifacts are in place. I’m running deck resolution first; the main thing to verify is that the drawn Storm lands at the expected live hand index after Peddler.
Deck resolution reported complete. I’m polling that command cleanly, then I’ll run the board CLI against the resolved damage counters.
Now I’m resolving the board. If any movement path is invalid, I’ll adjust only that movement and rerun.
The board CLI rejected one eastern raider move as distance 5. I’m shortening that move to a legal pressure hex while leaving the kills, center flip, and recruit plan intact.
The movement file is corrected. I’m rerunning board resolution against the same deck result.
Board resolution succeeded after the movement correction. I’m inspecting the result now so the strict commit summary matches the actual computed removals and captures.
The result killed both damaged P2 units and recruited one raider; the Storm damage was produced but not needed for legal attacks this turn. I’m committing with strict win validation now.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01 --deck-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-009.deck.result.json --board-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-009.board.result.json --summary \"P1 played Blast, Peddler, and the drawn Storm, bought Training, killed P2's damaged guardian and marksman, flipped center-center, advanced eastern raiders, and recruited a raider.\" --reasoning \"Blast preserved an action while producing damage, Peddler drew Storm and added enough money with the treasures for a 5-cost buy, and Storm added tactical damage even though the available legal attacks only needed printed damage to finish the wounded targets. Training was the best rush-consistent buy at 5 because it keeps action tempo while adding permanent attack scaling for future board pressure. On board, the marksman moved from 7,4 to 6,5 to flip center-center and used ranged fire to remove the 3 HP guardian. The west raider stayed adjacent to P2's 2 HP marksman and killed it, removing P2's center-west defender. The eastern raiders advanced toward the center and northeast lane while keeping center-northeast controlled, and moving the home raider freed a spawn. With six controlled centers, P1 gained 8 supply, reached 10, and recruited one raider at 10,1 to rebuild the unit lead and sustain pressure.\" --strict-win"

item.text:

P1 played `blast`, `peddler`, and the drawn `storm`, bought `training`, killed P2’s damaged guardian and marksman, flipped `center-center`, advanced eastern raiders, and recruited one raider.
